name: script_basenames
comment: table of scriptN.cod/.bas/.var/.dic/.deb names (16-byte stride)

name: font_ascii_map
comment: dialogue font ASCII map (DS:0x7802)

name: resource_handle_resolve
comment: SEG 0x4b9:0x190: resolve resource handle AX->loaded segment. bx=AX<<3 (8-byte entries); if fs:[bx+2]&3 set, ds=fs:[bx]=resource segment, si=0, ax=1(loaded) else ax=0. Resource handle table = fs base, {seg u16, flags u16, ...} per 8-byte entry

name: resource_name_table_full
comment: FS:0x0c04 complete 16-byte resource-filename table indexed by resource ID: 0 fupcom.spr, 1 nosound.drv, 2-6 script1.{cod,bas,var,dic,deb}, 7 radio, 8-12 buffer, 13-14 bappel, 15 btv, 16 borxx, 17 bcarte, 18 bhyper, 19 bpol, 20 aphyper, 21 appol, 22-36 worlds (black..cyber), 37-41 script2 set, 42 dnsdb.drv, 43 corpo, 44 carte, 45 bigark, 46+ cyber2/3 + sub-levels + script3/4/5

name: resource_release
comment: SEG 0x4b9:0xf8: release resource handle AX if loaded. bx=AX<<3; if fs:[bx+2]&3 (loaded flag) set, call 0x529c (actual free). Resource table entry = 8 bytes {segment u16@+0, flags u16@+2 (bits0-1=loaded), ...}. Counterpart to resource_handle_resolve 0x5320

name: resource_free_inner
comment: SEG 0x4b9:0x10c: actual resource release (segment dealloc) called by resource_release once the loaded-flag check passes

name: resource_load_by_id
comment: SEG 0x1ce:0x59b: load resource by ID AX. ds=fs; si=0x0c04+AX*16 (FS:0x0c04 name-table 16-byte entry = filename); call 0x28ca (lookup/already-loaded), lcall 0x4b9:0 (alloc slot), call 0x2abb (open+read file into segment). Returns ax=success. THE entry point for loading worlds/scripts: e.g. load venusia = resource_load_by_id(25). Called per-resource by vm_resource_profile_select 0x53da

name: resource_name_lookup
comment: SEG 0x1ce:0x5ea: given DS:SI = FS:0x0c04 name-table entry, resolve/check the resource; returns ebp (0 = skip/already-resolved)

name: resource_file_load
comment: SEG 0x1ce:0x7db: open the resource filename (via gs-relative path builder 0x2693) + read the file into the allocated resource segment; the loaded world/.ext data then lives at the resource segment (see resource_handle_resolve 0x5320)

name: resource_file_size
comment: loaded resource's file size (dword), read from the DOS FindFirst DTA at +0x1a during resource_file_load 0x2abb; also mirrored at GS:0x0A92. Bounds the read of the world/.ext data into its segment

name: resource_load_findfirst_size
comment: resource_file_load inner: int21h 0x2f00 (get DTA), 0x4e00 (FindFirst by built path) -> file size from DTA+0x1a into GS:0x0A8E/0A92, then int21h 0x3d00 (open) + read the bytes into the resource segment

name: entity_object_table
comment: 32-byte object/entity records indexed by object-id<<5 (gs-relative). Fields observed: +0x00 flags (|0x83 active/type bits, from resource [si]&4), +0x04 data offset + +0x06 data segment (far ptr into a loaded resource segment), +0x0c/+0x0e first two data words (position?), +0x14/+0x16 initial backup of +0xc/+0xe (reset-to values). Populated by entity_object_populate 0x40d0

name: entity_object_populate
comment: populate entity_object_table[AX] from resource handle DX: di=0x6212+AX<<5; lcall resource_handle_resolve 0x5320 (ds=resource seg,si); bound-check bp<[si+2]; flags=([si]&4)|0x83 -> +0x00; unpack packed dword ds:[bp<<2+si] into a far ptr (low nibble=extra si offset, >>4=segment delta added to ds) -> +0x04/+0x06; copy data words -> +0x0c/+0x0e, init +0x14/+0x16. First decode of the object-instance system

name: entity_object_populate_from_src
comment: variant of entity_object_populate: fills entity_object_table[AX] (si=0x6212+AX<<5) from a caller-supplied source ES:DI instead of a resolved resource. Identical record layout (+0x00 flags=([di]&4)|0x83, +0x04/+0x06 unpacked far ptr, +0x0c/+0x0e data words, +0x14/+0x16 init backups) - two independent routines agreeing CONFIRMS the 32-byte entity record structure

name: entity_get_flags
comment: AX=object id -> AX = flags word gs:[0x6212+id<<5] (entity record +0x00). Simple getter into entity_object_table

name: entity_flag_state_transition
comment: AX=object id: read flags gs:[0x6212+id<<5]; if active (bit7/0x80 set, js) AND bit0(0x01) set, clear bit0 and set bit1 (al=(al&0xfe)|2); store back. Per-object flag state machine - entity flags +0x00 bits: 0x80=active, 0x01/0x02 = state (0x83 init = active+state0+bit1). Transitions state0->state-advance
